a. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the
account and make the necessary adjustment immediately;

reconciliation of cashbook balances and accounting records before closing
the book on year- end; and,

the Audit Team the validated deposit slip of the deposit pertinent to the
aforesaid undeposited collections for audit.

a. Communicate/coordinate with the depository banks to obtain relevant
information on the unrecorded Credit Memos.
b. Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to forward the documents pertaining to the
unrecorded disbursements of ₱1,470.00 and investigate the circumstances
on why it was not submitted to the Accounting Office; and,
c. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to account for
the book reconciling items.

We recommended that Management:
a. Designate or appoint General Services Officer (GSO) who shall be
responsible for managing and overseeing supplies and inventory, including
report management;
b. Prepare and submit SSMI and RPCI using the prescribed format for
efficient reconciliation of records, and following the inventory procedures
and policies;
c. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately reconcile their inventory records, and if warranted, create
adjusting entries to account any discrepancy;
d. Maintain the Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Card to effectively monitor
the receipt and release of inventory; and,
e. Conduct semi-annual physical count and timely submit complete RPCI to
the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management:
a. Enjoin the Inventory Committee to identify the cause of the variance
between the physically counted properties and the Accounting records to
make the necessary reconciliation and adjustments;
b. Enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in their physical count, not only
the movable properties and the local road networks but also other
immovable properties of the LGU and submit the complete RPCPPE to the
Audit Team; and,
c. Create a committee composed of one personnel each from the Treasurer’s
Office, Municipal Engineer’s Office and Accounting Office who shall
reconcile their respective records of PPE and at the same time maintain
updated and complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to liquidate fund transfers once projects
are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amount from completed
projects and require the appropriate recording in the books of accounts.
Return of excess funds shall be done immediately if the other party requires
such action, otherwise reclassify the amount involved to its proper account;
b. Require the concerned departments to implement projects which remain
unimplemented as of year-end and secure a copy of MOA or Trust
Agreement from source agencies. Henceforth, ensure that the COA Audit
Team is always furnished a copy of MOA or Trust Agreement for validation
of transactions involving Trust Fund;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a correcting entry in the
books for the account with a negative balance amounting to ₱75,520.00; and,
d. Observe strict compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013, and the
requirements and intent in the Approved Memorandum of Agreement and
monitor timely liquidation of expenditures/disbursements.

direct LGU Drivers to accomplish every Driver’s Trip Tickets with complete data; and

ensure that only fuel consumptions of LGU Official/Personnel who comply with the requirement of indicating a valid purpose of travel in the driver’s trip ticket fuel consumption be charged against government funds to avoid any disallowance in audit; and

Recommendation:

Cruz and Agao-ao, respectively, for falsifying the signatures of Barangay Secretary, Barangay Nutrition Scholar, President and Treasurer of the Day Care Service Parents Groups of the Lovely Day Care Center.  Relative to the other DCWs who submitted fabricated attendance sheets, we also recommended that unless the concerned DCWs can present sufficient documents, appropriate cases should be filed against them and other officials who will be determined to have been remiss in the performance of their duties.

Recommendation:
Considering the fact that the Notices of Disallowances (NDs) have been served already, it is recommended that the DCWs and Mrs. Marcela Bornilla, MSWDO of Ragay should refund the entire amount or make their appeals. Further, payment of the appeal fee should be made first before the submission of the appeal.
observations.reason: The disallowance issued by COA is under appeal by the MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a) Start the process and implement appropriate steps that would lead to the closure of the open dump and would result to switching to sanitary landfill; b) Intensify efforts to achieve and embrace the full concept of Ecological solid waste management and adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003, c) Discontinue the practice of collecting non-segregated  waste and come up with measures  that would help its cause. Encouraging waste segregation at source will also be beneficial in transitioning from an open dumpsite to a sanitary land fill; and d) Adopt scheduling method/scheme which would  allow  the collection of different types of wastes on different days. We also recommended that Management require the ESWM Board to speed up the revision and/or creation of their new ESWM Plan to adapt to the current needs and demands in addressing environmental problems.

observations.reason: Concerned  official did not make any comment on this matter.
